headline: How Factory Closure and Asset Impairment Charges Will Impact Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Investors
summary: Stanley Black & Decker has begun closing its long-standing New Britain, Connecticut factory, cutting close to 300 measuring-tape jobs amid reduced demand and recording US$22.7 million in first-quarter asset impairment charges tied to exiting a Tools and Outdoor product line and an associated plant closure. These layoffs and footprint changes come just after the company reported higher quarterly sales year over year and raised its 2026 GAAP EPS guidance following the sale of Consolidated...

headline: Emerson Q2 Earnings in Line, Sales Miss on Middle East Disruptions
summary: EMR posts modest Q2 growth as pricing and demand offset geopolitical headwinds, while backlog strength and orders signal momentum into a stronger second half.

headline: Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Just Reclaimed the 200-Day Moving Average
summary: Is it a good or bad thing when a stock surpasses resistance at the 200-day simple moving average?
